caption:
"Meet CPC - Sororities in the Women's Collegiate Panhellenic Council!
The Collegiate Panhellenic Council (CPC) at UIC consists of 4 sororities: Alpha Sigma Tau, Chi Sigma Omega, Delta Phi Epsilon, and Phi Sigma Sigma. Each sorority holds its own set of values and pillars, but all come together to reach the same goal of creating a safe and welcoming space for all women. From lifelong friendships to leadership opportunities to academic support, CPC has so much to offer, not just for your undergraduate career, but for a lifetime. Formal recruitment is quickly approaching on August 31, so make sure to follow @uic.cpc and register to find your future home! 
#gocpc #panhellenic #UICFSL".
caption_raw:
135 likes, 3 comments - uicfsl August 23, 2023: "Meet CPC - Sororities in the Women's Collegiate Panhellenic Council!
The Collegiate Panhellenic Council (CPC) at UIC consists of 4 sororities: Alpha Sigma Tau, Chi Sigma Omega, Delta Phi Epsilon, and Phi Sigma Sigma. Each sorority holds its own set of values and pillars, but all come together to reach the same goal of creating a safe and welcoming space for all women. From lifelong friendships to leadership opportunities to academic support, CPC has so much to offer, not just for your undergraduate career, but for a lifetime. Formal recruitment is quickly approaching on August 31, so make sure to follow @uic.cpc and register to find your future home! 
#gocpc #panhellenic #UICFSL".

caption:
"The UIC Fraternity and Sorority Life (FSL) community is proud to present MEET FSL, a fun interactive signature showcase of fraternities and sororities on campus, where active members are excited to meet you!
Join us Wednesday, September 6, 11am-2pm in the Student Center East (SCE) Illinois Room. Fraternities and Sororities will have tables to share their information, and they'll be delighted to answer your questions on how to join and what makes their organization unique. Stay for the Step, Stroll, & Salute, with neat performances throughout the afternoon! We can't wait to meet all of you MEET FSL!
#UICFSL #UICLife #ThisIsUIC".

caption:
"As we transition to summer, the staff across all Student Engagement units are excited to welcome in all incoming first-year and transfer students for Orientation, where a leading question is, "What will you make time for?" as you consider your level of connection and engagement across campus. We're thrilled to walk with you on your journey to explore new experiences, expand your connections, and excel in your personal development! ➡️ Check out a few images of staff preparing to guide new Flames to campus, with special thanks to @uicorientation and all OLs who will truly lead the experience! 🔥
#ThisIsUIC #UICBound #UICFSL #UICLife @uiccsi @uiccocl @uicslce @uicfsl @uiclife".

caption:
"UICFSL goes abroad! How did everyone enjoy their spring break? 🤩 We're thrilled to share a feature of the intersection between academics, cultural enrichment, and collective representation, with these 5 outstanding FSL student leaders showcasing their engagement and organization pride. This group was selected for a UIC study abroad program in London with the Honors College, linked to HON 124, entitled "POP! Art and Culture 1960s Britain," where students learned through the synergy of Pop Art, Fashion, and Culture in London during the 60s. Throughout the study abroad portion, students visited many museums and historical sites connected to the 60s and the Beatles. For many, it was the first time leaving the US or traveling in Europe! (Pictured L-R: Alaa Mohamed, President of Chi Sigma Omega; Natalie Malczewski, Member of Chi Sigma Omega; Felix Celestino, Vice President of Phi Kappa Psi; Emma Johnson, Member of Alpha Sigma Tau; & Berenika Lach, Member of Phi Sigma Simga).
Share your stories with us!
#UICFSL".

107 likes, 2 comments - uicfsl March 28, 2023: "UICFSL goes abroad! How did everyone enjoy their spring break? 🤩 We're thrilled to share a feature of the intersection between academics, cultural enrichment, and collective representation, with these 5 outstanding FSL student leaders showcasing their engagement and organization pride. This group was selected for a UIC study abroad program in London with the Honors College, linked to HON 124, entitled "POP! Art and Culture 1960s Britain," where students learned through the synergy of Pop Art, Fashion, and Culture in London during the 60s. Throughout the study abroad portion, students visited many museums and historical sites connected to the 60s and the Beatles. For many, it was the first time leaving the US or traveling in Europe! (Pictured L-R: Alaa Mohamed, President of Chi Sigma Omega; Natalie Malczewski, Member of Chi Sigma Omega; Felix Celestino, Vice President of Phi Kappa Psi; Emma Johnson, Member of Alpha Sigma Tau; & Berenika Lach, Member of Phi Sigma Simga).
Share your stories with us!
#UICFSL".

caption:
"We warmly welcome all FSL community members to join us in honoring outstanding achievements at the FSL Awards 2023, "Blossoming Through Our Memories," on Monday, April 17.
Nominations remain open thru Sunday, April 2, at go.uic.edu/fslawards2023, and at the link in our bio. 
After submitting your thoughtful nominations, use the same website to locate the Awards RSVP to secure your seat for the dinner & awards ceremony. All attendees must RSVP to enjoy the in-person ceremony on April 17 in the SCE Illinois Room!
#UICFSL
#FSLAwards2023
http://go.uic.edu/fslawards2023".

caption:
"UIC Fraternity and Sorority Life (FSL) welcomes ALL students, plus faculty, staff, advisors, & friends, to Meet FSL! Our signature kickoff event of the year, Wednesday, Sept 7, 11a-3p in the Student Center East (SCE) Illinois Room (3rd floor). 
* Meet active student leaders from all Fraternity and Sorority organizations on campus, learn about their missions and values in action -- and how to JOIN, & discover the vibrancy of our community engagement! 
* Follow us here, on our web (fsl.uic.edu), and refer to our link in our bio anytime!
* go.uic.edu/MeetFSL
#UICFSL #ThisIsUIC".

caption:

caption:

go.uic.edu/ReachYourGoals

The full month of Wellness Center programs is found at: go.uic.edu/TakeCareFlames

#TakeCareFlames #UIC #UICFSL".

caption:
"FSL! ❤ Participate in the UIC Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Commemoration Week 2022, January 17-21. This year's theme is "IT STARTS WITH ME: Creating a Beloved Community". Kickoff commemoration week with the UIC MLK Day of Service on January 17 (go.uic.edu/mlksignup) and participate in a variety of other events all week long, including the MLK Book Club and A Critical Conversation with Michelle Alexander, January 18. 

For details, visit: go.uic.edu/MLK2022

Tremendous thanks @uiccsi
#UICMLK2022".

caption:
"Juneteenth - Freedom Day: Juneteenth is the celebration and commemoration that enslaved people were free and the war had ended, while the Emancipation Proclamation had been official two and a half years prior.  Federal troops arrived in Galveston, TX, June 19, 1865, to ensure all were freed. The following year, the first of what became the annual "Jubilee Day" was organized, and celebrations have endured and evolved across communities, with gatherings, education, service, & reflection that honors its tremendous importance. 

➡️ Get engaged with @aaccatuic & Freedom Celebration events all throughout this Saturday, June 19, including the 1:00pm Screening of "Black-ish: Juneteenth"; A Day to Volunteer, supporting the Urban Growers Collective; & Virtual Cookout.

✅ Register at aacc.uic.edu

#Juneteenth #ThisIsUIC".

Federal troops arrived in Galveston, TX, June 19, 1865, to ensure all were freed. The following year, the first of what became the annual "Jubilee Day" was organized, and celebrations have endured and evolved across communities, with gatherings, education, service, & reflection that honors its tremendous importance.

caption:
"Happy Pride Month! With much to honor and celebrate, for inclusion of all of our FSL siblings, we take time to recognize the past to ensure a future of inclusion, justice, and love. 

On June 28, 1969, a police raid at the Stonewall Inn bar in Greenwich Village, New York against LGBTQ+ people led to major action against the NYPD and their discriminatory practices towards queer people. Stormé DeLarverie resisted arrest, and Marsha P. Johnson is credited with throwing the first brick. Led by Black and Brown trans and queer folx, the rebellion lasted six days. Stonewall was an uprising and a watershed moment in LGBTQ+ history. In 1970, on the one year anniversary of Stonewall, the first ever Pride parade took place in NYC as an act of defiance, and Pride parades and celebrations, over 50 years later, have grown. There are great moments to celebrate communities and the progress made, always remembering where things have started and ensuring continued movement towards equity.

#FSLSiblings #UICFSL #LoveIsLove".
